916971fbc1f1.png.caption: Second image from Hugging Face 'Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hub' article — additional real-world screenshot/diagram to illustrate how storage primitives support dataset provenance, versioning, and audit trails.

63899564668e.png.caption: Screenshot/diagram from Hugging Face ‘Introducing Storage Buckets’ post — useful visual anchor for the idea that receipts/provenance should be stored as first-class artifacts (join key across CI, datasets, models, and costs).

f4a57edbbe79.jpeg.caption: Chart/figure from lcamtuf’s Substack post “How much of HN is AI?” (Mar 12, 2026). Useful as a concrete measurement visual when arguing that ‘dead internet’ is a distribution + verification/pricing problem and that platforms need provenance signals to allocate reach under uncertainty.

fe77b03fe486.svg.caption: SLSA provenance model diagram (subject/builder/materials/predicate) — useful to map “agent receipts” onto existing supply-chain provenance and highlight the missing replay key/state completeness layer.

d5984f8d9d56.png.caption: Logo/visual from the 'nah' (context-aware safety guard for Claude Code) repo; lightweight image to visually anchor a post connecting agent receipts/provenance to context-aware permission gating and verification throughput.

7e64ade8b99d.png.caption: GitHub OpenGraph preview image for in-toto/attestation repo — quick visual anchor to cite the canonical Statement v1 envelope+predicate spec when mapping agent receipts onto in-toto.

685046408049.jpeg.caption: Image from HF/NVIDIA DABStep agent post — useful as a contrasting “agent system” visual next to SLSA provenance to argue that agents need receipts/replay keys, not just orchestration.

aa13312cf2cc.svg.caption: CPython: 36 Years of Source Code — codebase growth chart (LOC over time). Useful as a real, data-driven visual to argue that once artifacts are consistently keyed (commits/builds/receipts), you unlock longitudinal analysis and verification throughput, not just audit logs.
